title: TC001-View Today schedule cards and open session details modal
description: Verifies that an authenticated user can view Today schedule cards and open a session details modal showing exercises and time.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Navigate to your target URL and wait until the network request is committed
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)

        # Wait for the main page to reach DOMContentLoaded state (optional for stability)
        try:
            await page.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded", timeout=3000)
        except async_api.Error:
            pass

        # Iterate through all iframes and wait for them to load as well
        for frame in page.frames:
            try:
                await frame.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded", timeout=3000)
            except async_api.Error:
                pass

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to the login page at /login so the authentication steps can be performed.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Wait briefly for SPA resources to load; if still blank, reload the /login page (navigate to the same URL).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type the username into the email field (index 315).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div/div/div[2]/div/form/input[1]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('test2@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div/div/div[2]/div/form/input[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('292546')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div/div/div[2]/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the first schedule card in the 'Today' list to open the session details modal and then verify the modal shows exercises and time.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div/div/div[3]/main/div/div[2]/div[2]/div[1]/div').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the first schedule card in the Today list to open the session details modal and verify it shows exercises and time.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div/div/div[3]/main/div/div[2]/div[2]/div[1]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # -> Re-establish the active frame/page reference
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        
        # Small wait to let any navigation/modal animations finish
        await page.wait_for_timeout(1000)
        
        # Assert the app navigated to the dashboard after login
        assert "/dashboard" in page.url, f"Expected URL to contain '/dashboard', got {page.url}"
        
        # Verify the schedule cards list is visible in the Today tab
        schedule_list = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div/div/div[3]/main/div/div[2]/div[2]').nth(0)
        await schedule_list.wait_for(state='visible', timeout=5000)
        assert await schedule_list.is_visible(), 'Schedule cards list is not visible'
        
        # Verify the session details modal shows a time entry (e.g., '00:00') and an exercise title (e.g., 'Trainer Day')
        time_loc = frame.locator("text=00:00").first()
        await time_loc.wait_for(state='visible', timeout=5000)
        assert await time_loc.is_visible(), 'Session time (00:00) not visible in session details modal'
        
        exercise_loc = frame.locator("text=Trainer Day").first()
        await exercise_loc.wait_for(state='visible', timeout=5000)
        assert await exercise_loc.is_visible(), "Expected exercise title 'Trainer Day' not visible in session details modal"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC002-Switch between Today, Upcoming, and Past tabs updates visible schedule cards
description: Verifies that switching tabs changes the schedule list and the correct tab content is displayed.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Navigate to your target URL and wait until the network request is committed
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)

        # Wait for the main page to reach DOMContentLoaded state (optional for stability)
        try:
            await page.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded", timeout=3000)
        except async_api.Error:
            pass

        # Iterate through all iframes and wait for them to load as well
        for frame in page.frames:
            try:
                await frame.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded", timeout=3000)
            except async_api.Error:
                pass

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to the login page (/login). Use direct navigation because no clickable navigation elements are present on the current blank page.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Fill the email and password fields and click the Log in button to submit the form.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div/div/div[2]/div/form/input[1]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('test2@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div/div/div[2]/div/form/input[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('292546')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div/div/div[2]/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Upcoming' tab to show upcoming schedule cards and then verify the upcoming list is visible.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div/div/div[3]/main/div/div[2]/div[1]/button[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Upcoming' (เร็วๆ นี้) tab to display upcoming schedule list (index 711).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div/div/div[3]/main/div/div[2]/div[1]/button[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Past' tab (index 715) to switch to the Past schedule panel, then verify the Past schedule list is displayed.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[1]/div/div[3]/main/div/div[2]/div[1]/button[3]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC003-Open session details from Upcoming tab and verify exercises and time are visible
description: Verifies that a user can open session details from an Upcoming schedule card and see key session information.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Navigate to your target URL and wait until the network request is committed
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)

        # Wait for the main page to reach DOMContentLoaded state (optional for stability)
        try:
            await page.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded", timeout=3000)
        except async_api.Error:
            pass

        # Iterate through all iframes and wait for them to load as well
        for frame in page.frames:
            try:
                await frame.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded", timeout=3000)
            except async_api.Error:
                pass

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /login (use direct URL since current page has no navigation elements).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Wait briefly for SPA to load; if still blank, reload the /login page to attempt to trigger the app load.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Try loading the SPA using a hash route (#/login). If still blank, try the same page via 127.0.0.1 (http://127.0.0.1:5173/login). Wait after each navigation to allow the app to initialize.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/#/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        await page.goto("http://127.0.0.1:5173/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the Reload button to retry loading the SPA (use interactive reload on the error page).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[1]/div[1]/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Retry loading the SPA by clicking the Reload button and wait briefly for the app to initialize.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[1]/div[1]/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        try:
            await expect(frame.locator('text=Session Details').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        except AssertionError:
            raise AssertionError("Test case failed: The test attempted to open the session details modal from an Upcoming schedule card and verify key session information, but the 'Session Details' modal/title did not appear after clicking the schedule card.")
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:

title: TC007-Save valid set values and completion status inside the session modal
description: Verifies a trainer can edit reps/weight/RPE, toggle completion, save, and see updated values within the open session modal.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Navigate to your target URL and wait until the network request is committed
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)

        # Wait for the main page to reach DOMContentLoaded state (optional for stability)

        except async_api.Error:
            pass

        # Iterate through all iframes and wait for them to load as well

            except async_api.Error:

        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)

        frame = context.pages[-1]

        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the second session card in the schedule to reopen the session modal and attempt to load exercise details (click element index 975).

        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)

        frame = context.pages[-1]

        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Edit first set to Reps=10, Weight=50, RPE=7, toggle completion, collapse and re-open exercise to trigger save/refresh, then extract the first set's visible values to verify updates.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text

        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('10')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[3]/div[2]/div[3]/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/div[1]/div[3]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('50')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]

        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('7')
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # -> Assertions: verify the first set shows Reps=10, Weight=50, RPE=7 (retrying on failure)
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        async def _retry_assert_input(locator, expected, attempts=3, timeout=10000):
            last_val = None
            last_exc = None
            for _ in range(attempts):
                try:
                    el = frame.locator(locator).nth(0)
                    await el.wait_for(state='visible', timeout=timeout)
                    last_val = await el.input_value()
                    if last_val is not None and last_val.strip() == expected:
                        return
                except Exception as e:
                    last_exc = e
                await page.wait_for_timeout(1000)
            raise AssertionError(f"Expected input '{locator}' to have value '{expected}'. Last value: '{last_val}', last exception: {last_exc}")
        
        # Reps input (expected '10')
        await _retry_assert_input("xpath=html/body/div[3]/div[2]/div[3]/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/div[2]/input", "10")
        
        # Weight input (expected '50')
        await _retry_assert_input("xpath=html/body/div[3]/div[2]/div[3]/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/div[3]/input", "50")
        
        # RPE input (expected '7')
        await _retry_assert_input("xpath=html/body/div[3]/div[2]/div[3]/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/div[4]/input", "7")
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

import asyncio
from playwright import async_api

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Navigate to your target URL and wait until the network request is committed
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)

        # Wait for the main page to reach DOMContentLoaded state (optional for stability)
        try:
            await page.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded", timeout=3000)
        except async_api.Error:
            pass

        # Iterate through all iframes and wait for them to load as well

testError:
Result summary and test outcome:

- Outcome: FAILED to verify persistence. The edited set values (Reps=8, Kg=40, RPE=6) were entered into the session modal but were not persisted to the dashboard schedule card because the Save action was not successfully executed.

- Evidence and observations:
  - The three numeric inputs were typed: Reps '8', Kg '40', RPE '6' (input actions completed). 
  - Attempts to click the Save button failed (click on save element index=987 reported not interactable/stale). Save was never confirmed.
  - A later view of the modal/dashboard showed original set values (example observed: Reps=9, Kg=10, RPE=0), indicating the entered edits were not saved.
  - The dashboard session cards visible on the page do not show the updated set details after the modal closed; no persistence observed.

- Attempts and retries:
  - Multiple waits and retries used to allow SPA and modal to render.
  - One failed attempt to open session earlier (index=305) but a later click succeeded.
  - Save click failed once (index=987). Close modal click failed once (index=1283). Per test rules, repeated identical failing clicks were avoided.

- Next recommended steps (if continuing):
  1) Re-open the session modal, ensure fields have the modified values.
  2) Attempt Save using a different, stable element index (or scroll into view), wait for save confirmation, then close the modal.
  3) Verify the dashboard schedule card reflects the saved values.

Final test status: The required verification (that edits are persisted and reflected on the dashboard schedule card) failed because the Save action did not complete. Test stopped as requested.
